thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. The only supported exploit candidate is still ETH channel on ETH/1h, but soft 70/30 balance and concentration rules argue for another exploration turn rather than chaining ETH refinement. Recent journal history already includes heavy ETH-centric exploit logic plus fresh BTC/SOL daily exploration, so rotate away from both ETH/1h and daily lanes while staying inside the supported universe. Target DOGE 4h and demand 3 materially different continuation specs around negative-funding acceptance rather than reversal: one compression breakout-hold above a medium anchor, one base-break retest continuation that requires hold after reclaim of the broken range, and one trend-state expansion branch that only arms after consolidation resolves with directional-strength confirmation. No reclaim/snapback fade, no failed-continuation ETH clone, and no daily trend-resumption copy.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 18, and max_drawdown_pct <= 10.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.02, or trending regime profit_factor < 1.00.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: the system should remain explore-heavy, ETH/1h is the only valid exploit lane and was just used recently, and recent concentration already covers ETH-centric exploitation plus daily exploration, so DOGE/4h is the clean supported rotation.
